summary:
All specs are in place and syntactically valid. Note: the repo pins Ruby 4.0.6 (not installed in this environment), so I verified syntax with an available interpreter but could not execute the suite; the specs are written to fail until Build implements the design, as intended.

## Summary

I wrote executable Minitest specs capturing the observable behavior of the **Live pipeline status summary** feature, aligning them to the iteration-2 technical design (the pre-existing iteration-1 specs had drifted from it). In `test/lib/pipelines/status_summary_test.rb` (moved from `test/services/` to mirror the PORO's `app/lib` home, and replacing the stale copy) I specified the `Pipelines::StatusSummary.for(pipeline)` domain value — its `text`/`tone`/`phase_label` contract and every resolution branch: not-started (R10), one active step naming phase/step/activity with the iteration shown only on the 2nd+ pass and a type-verb fallback (R3/R4), two steps both named (R5), three-or-more collapsing to a phase-plus-count (R6), human-gate and escalation awaiting-human wording (R7), completed (R8), failed-names-phase-and-step (R9), canceled-in-a-muted-tone (R11), the never-blank single-line totality guard and running-but-idle catch-all (R12), tone-is-a-known-`TONE_CLASSES`-key for a11y (R17), and plain-language/no-internal-codes (R13). I updated `broadcast_status_test.rb` to target the stable `dom_id(pipeline, :summary)` and assert claim/progress/complete each refresh it (R14/R16); extended `manager_tick_test.rb` with the essential gate-wait and escalation broadcasts (the seam where only phase/pipeline status changes, R7/R14); and rewrote the `test/integration/pipeline_live_status_test.rb` request specs for true-state-on-load, the polite live region, stream subscription, and the compact list surface matching the detail surface (R1/R2/R15/R17/R18) — using integration tests since the repo has no system-test base class configured. No production code was written.
